name: Spicy Seafood Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 Fresh lemon grass stalks, coarsely chopped
2 Fresh Serrano chiles, stemmed, seeded, and chopped
24 medium Mussels or clams in shells
1 tbsp Slivered fresh red hot chile
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp. Refrigerate the shrimp and reserve the shells.
2. Heat oil in a soup pot or large, heavy saucepan over high heat.
3. Add shrimp shells and sauté until they turn bright pink.
4. Add fish stock, lemon grass or lime zest, leaves, galangal or ginger, and chiles. Bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes.
6. Strain the broth through a fine sieve into a clean soup pot (can be refrigerated covered until cool, then tightly covered and stored up to 3 days).
7. Return strained soup to high heat over high heat. Add mussels or clams, cover, and cook until shells open about 2 minutes.
8. Remove mussels or clams and discard top shell. Return to broth.
9. Add shrimp and cook until opaque about 2 minutes.
10. Reduce heat to low, stir in lime juice, fish sauce, cilantro, slivered chile, green onion, and salt to taste. Simmer for 1 minute.
11. Ladle into preheated soup bowls, garnish with lime slices, leaves, and blossoms, and serve immediately.
Serves 6 as a soup course or 3-4 as a main dish.
